title: archidendron kunstleri subsp. kunstleri
intro: Archidendron kunstleri subsp. kunstleri, also known as the Burmese tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Research has shown that it has various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections. The plant's leaves and bark can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal purposes.
